missing_memo.reason: Heavy component with video playback, keyboard shortcuts, and multiple event listeners. Not wrapped in React.memo — re-renders on every parent update even when props haven't changed.
Renders a dynamic list of highlight items with scores and seek buttons. Not wrapped in React.memo — will re-render whenever parent (AIVisualizer) re-renders.
Heavy component with async video analysis, progress tracking, and multiple state updates. Not wrapped in React.memo.
Complex panel with multiple tabs, hooks, and child components. Not wrapped in React.memo despite being used as a tab content panel.
Renders a list of project items with dropdown menus. Not wrapped in React.memo — re-renders on every parent state change.
Top-level page component with many state handlers. Not wrapped in React.memo (acceptable for pages, but creates inline closures that cascade to children).
Complex page with project loading, workflow steps, and multiple interactive elements. Not wrapped in React.memo.
Heavy page component with lazy-loaded children, many state transitions, and complex data flow. Not wrapped in React.memo.
Inline sub-component (line 318) not wrapped in React.memo. Receives dispatch as prop but re-renders on every parent update.
Inline sub-component (line 364) not wrapped in React.memo. Stateless component that could easily be memoized.
Lazy-loaded panel component not wrapped in React.memo. Re-renders on every parent update.

inline_objects.pattern: style={{ padding: '40px 36px', position: 'relative', zIndex: 1, background: heroGradient }}
style={{ background: isDarkMode ? ... : ..., color: item.color }} (inside .map loop)
style={{ background: isDarkMode ? ... : ..., color: step.color }} (inside .map loop)
style={{ backgroundColor: statusCfg.color + '20', color: statusCfg.color, borderColor: statusCfg.color + '40' }} (inside .map loop)

missing_useMemo.pattern: projectActions() function creates a new array of action objects (with new closures) on every render. Called inside .map() for each project card — should be memoized with useMemo or useCallback.
getMenuItems() creates new array with new onClick closures on every call. Called inside .map() for each project — each project gets fresh function references.
totalHeight = tracks.reduce(...) computed on every render without useMemo. Could be expensive with many tracks.
totalWidth = duration / msPerPixel computed on every render without useMemo.
wordCount = currentScript?.content?.length || 0 — trivial but computed every render.
estimatedDuration = Math.ceil(wordCount / 3) — trivial but computed every render.
onEnded={() => {}} — empty inline function passed as prop, creates new reference every render.

missing_useCallback.pattern: handleThresholdChange = (value) => { ... } — not wrapped in useCallback, recreated every render, passed to Slider onValueChange.
handleTopNChange = (value) => { ... } — not wrapped in useCallback, recreated every render, passed to Slider onValueChange.
toggleConfig = (key) => { dispatchLocal(...) } — not wrapped in useCallback, recreated every render.
runAnalysis = async () => { ... } — not wrapped in useCallback, large async function recreated every render.
handleReAnalyze = () => { ... } — not wrapped in useCallback, recreated every render.
applyPlatformPreset = (platform) => { ... } — not wrapped in useCallback, recreated every render, passed to onClick handlers.
handleSaveBehaviorChange = (v) => { ... } — not wrapped in useCallback, passed to child components.
handleAutoSaveToggle = (checked) => { ... } — not wrapped in useCallback, passed to child components.
handleSegmentsChange = (newSegments) => { ... } — not wrapped in useCallback, passed to ScriptEditor.
getRelativeTime = (dateText) => { ... } — not wrapped in useCallback, called in render loops.
Inline arrow closures inside .map(): onOpen={() => { addRecentProject(...); navigate(...) }}, onDelete, onPreload — all create new function refs per project per render.
Inline arrow closures for ProjectsListView props: onOpenProject, onOpenEditor, onPreloadProject, onPreloadEditor — all recreated every render.
Inline onClick={() => setBatchMode(!batchMode)} — recreated every render.
Inline onClick={() => batchMode ? togglePlatformSelection(...) : applyPlatformPreset(...)} — recreated every render inside .map().

heavy_renders.issue: No React.memo. The togglePlay() and toggleFullscreen() functions (referenced in keyboard handler) are defined inside render but not wrapped in useCallback. The keyboard handler useEffect has [] deps but calls togglePlay() which is recreated — potential stale closure.

Three functions (toggleConfig, runAnalysis, handleReAnalyze) are not wrapped in useCallback. runAnalysis is an expensive async function recreated every render. The component uses memo() but internal callbacks defeat the purpose since parent StoryFab context changes frequently.
Not wrapped in React.memo. Two slider handlers (handleThresholdChange, handleTopNChange) recreated every render. The highlight list items have inline onClick handlers inside .map().

Uses memo() correctly, but passes inline arrow functions to StyleLengthConfig: onStyleChange={(style) => setConfig(...)}, onLengthChange={(length) => setConfig(...)} — these defeat memo on child components.

Uses memo() and useCallback properly for most handlers. However, totalHeight and totalWidth (lines 276-277) are computed on every render without useMemo. The keyboard handler useEffect (line 166) has many dependencies causing frequent re-subscription.

Renders ProjectCard in a .map() loop (line 93-111) with inline closures for onOpen, onDelete, onPreload — each project card gets new function references every render, defeating React.memo on ProjectCard.
Not wrapped in React.memo. Inline closures inside .map() loop (line 73: onClick={() => onOpenProject(project.id)}). The getMenuItems function creates new closures per project per render.

workflowSteps array (line 37-44) contains JSX elements created at module level (good), but the component has many inline style objects inside .map() loops (lines 199, 260, 261, 284) that create new objects on every render.

Not wrapped in React.memo. No useCallback on handleAnalyze. Has two inline sub-components (Title, Paragraph) defined inside the module — these create new component types on every module evaluation causing full remounts.

Uses memo() but passes many inline closures to child elements inside .map() loops (lines 142, 145, 180, 183, 204, 207, etc.). applyPlatformPreset is not wrapped in useCallback. The config spreading pattern ({...config, format: ...}) creates new objects on each click.
